Ukraine Conflict News





17 December 2025 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • Lethal Kinetic Strike, Dec. 17, 2025 SOUTHCOM 17 Dec 2025 -- On Dec. 17, at the direction of Secretary of War Pete Hegseth, Joint Task Force Southern Spear conducted a lethal kinetic strike on a vessel operated by a Designated Terrorist Organizations in international waters.
  • Readout of the Secretary-General's phone call with H.E. Nicolás Maduro Moros, the President of the Bolivarian Republic of Venezuela UN Secretary-General 17 Dec 2025
  • Rejected by Global Majority: Lavrov Calls Out US Actions in Caribbean Sputnik 17 Dec 2025 -- The US actions in the Caribbean are rejected by most countries around the world, Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov said on Wednesday.
  • Iran condemns US maritime blockade on Venezuela as state piracy IRNA 17 Dec 2025 -- The Iranian Foreign Ministry in a statement has condemned the United States' threats to unilaterally impose a maritime blockade on Venezuela, characterizing the move as a form of state-sponsored piracy.
  • Iran condemns US maritime blockade on Venezuela as 'state piracy' Press TV 17 Dec 2025 -- Iran's Foreign Ministry has issued a strongly worded statement condemning the United States' threats to unilaterally impose a maritime blockade on Venezuela, characterizing the move as a form of state-sponsored piracy.
  • Smith Demands Rules of Engagement, Execute Orders, Video of Follow-On Boat Strike Be Released and Hegseth to Publicly Testify House Armed Services Committee 17 Dec 2025 -- U.S. Representative Adam Smith (D-Wash.), Ranking Member of the House Armed Services Committee, released a statement in response to today's classified briefing with Admiral Frank "Mitch" Bradley on a follow-up strike in the SOUTHCOM region on September 2nd.
  • Meeks, Smith, Himes, Thompson, Crow & Omar Issue Statement Following House War Powers Resolution Vote House Armed Services Committee 17 Dec 2025 -- Representatives Gregory W. Meeks (D-N.Y.), Ranking Member of the House Foreign Affairs Committee; Adam Smith (D-Wash.), Ranking Member of the House Armed Services Committee; Jim Himes (D-Conn.), Ranking Member of the House Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence; Bennie G. Thompson (D-Miss.), Ranking Member of the House Homeland Security Committee; Jason Crow (D-Colo.), Ranking Member of the House Armed Services Subcommittee on Intelligence and Special Operations; and Ilhan Omar (D-Minn.) issued a statement following the vote on their War Powers Resolution to end the Trump administration's military strikes in the Caribbean and Pacific:
  • Chairman Mast Leads GOP Opposition Against Democratic Push to Shield Maduro's Venezuelan Cartel Regime U.S. House of Representatives Committee on Foreign Affairs 17 Dec 2025 -- Today, House Foreign Affairs Committee Chairman Brian Mast led Republicans in opposing a Democrat-led effort to preemptively disarm the president from defending the U.S. from Nicolás Maduro's terrorist regime.
  • Chairman Mast Leads GOP Debate Against Democrats' Ploy to Tie President Trump's Hands From Defending the U.S. From Cartel Terrorists U.S. House of Representatives Committee on Foreign Affairs 17 Dec 2025 -- Today, House Foreign Affairs Committee Chairman Brian Mast led debate on the House Floor in opposition to a Democrat-led resolution to block President Trump from defending the U.S. from designated cartel terrorists operating in the Western Hemisphere.
  • Trump orders 'total blockade' of sanctioned oil tankers to and from Venezuela Press TV 17 Dec 2025 -- US President Donald Trump has ordered a "total and complete blockade" of all sanctioned oil tankers entering or leaving Venezuela, directly targeting Caracas' main source of revenue.
  • Pressure mounts on U.S. Pentagon to release boat strike video People's Daily 17 Dec 2025 -- A growing bipartisan pressure in Congress is mounting on U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth to disclose a video of a controversial air raid on a vessel suspected of drug trafficking off the coast of Venezuela.

  • U.S. Forces Protect the Homeland with Aggressive Pursuit of ISIS in Syria CENTCOM 17 Dec 2025 -- U.S. and partner forces in Syria have conducted nearly 80 operations since July to eliminate terrorist operatives, including ISIS remnants, that posed a direct threat to the United States and interests abroad.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • The Evolution of Mortar Capabilities: How Enhancing 81mm Mortars is Shaping Modern Warfare Army News 17 Dec 2025 -- Engineers at Picatinny Arsenal's U.S. Army Combat Capabilities Development Command (DEVCOM) Armaments Center, are working to develop an enhanced 81mm mortar (E81) high-explosive (HE) mortar cartridge. This new cartridge is looking to significantly increase the maximum range and lethality of 81 mm mortar systems while retaining the hallmark portability of 81 mm mortar systems.
  • Scarlet Dragon Links Military, Industry to Test Artificial Intelligence for Warfighters DoD News 17 Dec 2025 -- On a cold December day, deep in a training area on Fort Bragg, North Carolina, soldiers, airmen, Marines and civilian industry partners came together to test the latest drone and counter-small unmanned aircraft systems technology, while rapidly sharing targeting data through the National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency's Maven Smart System.
  • Pathfinders drive TOC-L innovation US Air Force 17 Dec 2025 -- Pathfinder units are playing a crucial role in the Air Force's modernization of command and control, with the 752nd Operations Support Squadron at Tinker Air Force Base guiding the effort for the Tactical Operations Center-Light.
  • Nimitz Returns to Homeport US Navy 17 Dec 2025 -- The Nimitz class carrier USS Nimitz (CVN 68) returned to its homeport of Bremerton following nine months underway in the U.S. 3rd, 5th, and 7th Fleets, Dec. 16.
  • The Philippines and the U.S. Conduct Bilateral Maritime Cooperative Activity U.S. 7th Fleet 17 Dec 2025 -- The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) and the U.S. Navy, conducted a bilateral Maritime Cooperative Activity (MCA) within the Philippines' Exclusive Economic Zone, demonstrating a collective commitment to strengthen regional and international cooperation in support of a free and open Indo-Pacific, Dec. 15, 2025.
  • Risch on Priorities Included in FY2026 NDAA US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 17 Dec 2025 -- U.S. Senator Jim Risch (R-Idaho), chairman of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, released the following statements on his priorities that were included in the FY2026 National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA).
  • Department of War Statement on the Rotorcraft Operations Transparency and Oversight Reform Act DOD 17 Dec 2025

  • NATO Deputy Secretary General attends NATO - EU meeting on military mobility NATO 17 Dec 2025 -- On Wednesday 17 November 2025, at the Egmont Palace in Brussels, NATO Deputy Secretary General Radmila Shekerinska, together with the Chair of the Political and Security Committee of the European Union, Delphine Pronk, led an informal meeting of the North Atlantic Council and the Political and Security Committee, focusing on military mobility.
  • NATO innovation range advances defense technology integration in finland NATO Allied Land Command 17 Dec 2025 -- 8-10 December NATO Innovation Range hosted its first demonstration of advance defense technologies, focused on data fusion between uncrewed systems to national and NATO command and control networks. NATO Allied Land Command (LANDCOM) and NATO's Defense Industry, Innovation, and Armaments Division in partnership with the Defence Innovation Network Finland (DEFINE) ecosystem organized the event held at the DEFINE Innovation hub.

  • Expanded meeting of the Defence Ministry Board President of Russia 17 Dec 2025 -- The President took part in an expanded meeting of the Defence Ministry Board at the National Defence Control Centre of the Russian Federation.
  • Military Operation Goals Will Certainly be Achieved - Putin Sputnik 17 Dec 2025 -- Russia will certainly achieve the objectives of its special military operation in Ukraine, Russian President Vladimir Putin said on Wednesday.
  • Defence Ministry Board session, led by Supreme Commander-in-Chief of the Armed Forces of the Russian Federation Vladimir Putin, takes place in Moscow Russia MoD 17 Dec 2025 -- Today, an expanded meeting of the Board of the Defence Ministry of the Russian Federation took place at the National Centre for State Defence Control of the Russian Federation, where were presented the results of the activities of the Armed Forces of the Russian Federation in 2025 and were determined tasks for a further period.
  • Remarks of Report Russian Defence Minister Andrei Belousov reports on Expanded meeting of Defence Ministry Board Russia MoD 17 Dec 2025 -- Last year, at a meeting of the Board, the Ministry of Defence identified 10 priority areas for further actions. All of them still stay relevant in the near future. Today, we will summarise the results of the implementation and confirm the tasks for 2026.
  • Presenting Gold Star medals to special military operation participants President of Russia 17 Dec 2025 -- Following the extended meeting of the Defence Ministry Board, the President presented Gold Star medals of the Hero of the Russian Federation to participants of the special military operation.

  • Ramstein: What the Ukraine Defense Contact Group meetings delivered for Ukraine in 2025 Ukraine MOD 17 Dec 2025
  • Denys Shmyhal announces strengthened defence cooperation with Germany Ukraine MOD 17 Dec 2025
  • Minister of National Defence took part in a distance meeting of the Ukraine Defence Contact Group Lithuania MND 17 Dec 2025 -- On December 16 Minister of National Defence Robertas Kaunas took part in a distance meeting of the Ukraine Defense Contact Group where support to Ukraine, major needs of the country were on the agenda, as well as developments on the frontline and progress on peace talks.
  • The 32nd Ukraine Defence Contact Group Meeting Romania - Ministry of National Defence 17 Dec 2025 -- On Tuesday, December 16th, Acting Minister of National Defence, Radu Miruță, participated in the Ukraine Defence Contact Group Meeting, via videoconference. The Chief of Defence Staff, General Gheorghiță Vlad, also participated in the activity.
  • UK sends automated turrets and missiles to shoot down Russian drones, as part of £600 million air defence package for Ukraine UK MOD 17 Dec 2025 -- The UK has committed £600 million in air-defence capabilities, including cutting edge turrets that can shoot down Russian drones to support Ukraine through the winter.
  • Sergiy Boyev discusses support for Ukraine in 2026 with the state secretary of the Ministry of Defence of Latvia Ukraine MOD 17 Dec 2025
  • Government approved package of documents developed by Ministry of Defence to launch Defence City Ukraine MOD 17 Dec 2025
  • Norway to purchase vital F-16 ammunition and air defence for Ukraine Government of Norway 17 Dec 2025 -- The Norwegian Government is providing approximately NOK 3.2 billion in funding via the JUMPSTART mechanism (Joint Ukraine Multinational Program - Services, Training, and Articles Rapid Timeline) for the purchase of ammunition for F-16 fighter jets, laser-guided missiles for use by F-16s or ground-based air defence systems, and long-range missiles.

  • China, Singapore joint army training concludes PRC MND 17 Dec 2025 -- China and Singapore concluded a joint army training on Wednesday, the latest round of a bilateral exercise that has expanded over time from conventional security cooperation to highly complex urban counter-terrorism operations.
  • China's naval hospital ship Silk Road Ark leaves Jamaica for Barbados PRC MND 17 Dec 2025 -- The Chinese PLA Navy's hospital ship Silk Road Ark (Hull 867), which is on the Mission Harmony 2025, concluded its 12-day friendly visit to Jamaica on December 16, 2025, and left for Barbados, the next stop of its mission.
  • Foreign media reports alleged flight test of China's new medium transport aircraft; expert views it as complement to large Y-20 Global Times 17 Dec 2025 -- Images and footage that started to circulate online showing what was claimed to be China's new medium transport aircraft in a flight test on Tuesday have attracted attention from foreign media outlets. A Chinese expert said that China could use such a new aircraft to replace its current Y-9 series medium transport aircraft and complement the country's large transport aircraft, the Y-20.

  • Taipei Economic and Cultural Representative Office in the United States - Tactical Mission Network Software, Equipment, and Services DSCA 17 Dec 2025 -- The State Department has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Taipei Economic and Cultural Representative Office in the United States (TECRO) of Tactical Mission Network Software, Equipment, and Services and related equipment for an estimated cost of $1.01 billion. The Defense Security Cooperation Agency delivered the required certification notifying Congress.
  • Taipei Economic and Cultural Representative Office in the United States - AH-1W Helicopter Spare and Repair Parts DSCA 17 Dec 2025 -- The State Department has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Taipei Economic and Cultural Representative Office in the United States (TECRO) of AH-1W Helicopter Spare and Repair Parts and related equipment for an estimated cost of $96 million. The Defense Security Cooperation Agency delivered the required certification notifying Congress.
  • Taipei Economic and Cultural Representative Office in the United States - M109A7 Self-Propelled Howitzers DSCA 17 Dec 2025 -- The State Department has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Taipei Economic and Cultural Representative Office in the United States (TECRO) of M107A7 Self-Propelled Howitzers and related equipment for an estimated cost of $4.03 billion. The Defense Security Cooperation Agency delivered the required certification notifying Congress.
  • Taipei Economic and Cultural Representative Office in the United States - High Mobility Artillery Rocket Systems DSCA 17 Dec 2025 -- The State Department has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Taipei Economic and Cultural Representative Office in the United States (TECRO) of High Mobility Artillery Rocket Systems and related equipment for an estimated cost of $4.05 billion. The Defense Security Cooperation Agency delivered the required certification notifying Congress.
  • Taipei Economic and Cultural Representative Office in the United States - Tube-launched, Optically tracked, Wire-guided missile system DSCA 17 Dec 2025 -- The State Department has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Taipei Economic and Cultural Representative Office in the United States (TECRO) of Tube-launched, optically tracked, Wire-guided missile system and related equipment for an estimated cost of $353 million. The Defense Security Cooperation Agency delivered the required certification notifying Congress.
  • Taipei Economic and Cultural Representative Office in the United States - Javelin Missile System DSCA 17 Dec 2025 -- The State Department has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Taipei Economic and Cultural Representative Office in the United States (TECRO) of Javelin Missile System and related equipment for an estimated cost of $375 million. The Defense Security Cooperation Agency delivered the required certification notifying Congress.
  • Taipei Economic and Cultural Representative Office in the United States - Harpoon Missile Repair Follow-on Support DSCA 17 Dec 2025 -- The State Department has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Taipei Economic and Cultural Representative Office in the United States (TECRO) of Harpoon Missile Repair Follow-on Support and related equipment for an estimated cost of $91.4 million. The Defense Security Cooperation Agency delivered the required certification notifying Congress.
  • Taipei Economic and Cultural Representative Office in the United States - ALTIUS-700M and ALTIUS-600 Systems DSCA 17 Dec 2025 -- The State Department has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Taipei Economic and Cultural Representative Office in the United States (TECRO) of ALTIUS-700M and ALTIUS-600 Systems and related equipment for an estimated cost of $1.1 billion. The Defense Security Cooperation Agency delivered the required certification notifying Congress.
  • Taiwan made 'essential contributions' at Pax Silica summit: US official CNA 17 Dec 2025 -- Taiwan "was there and made essential contributions" at President Donald Trump's Pax Silica summit on silicon supply chain security issues, a senior American official said Tuesday (U.S. time).
  • Russia could support China attack on Taiwan: Taiwan security chief CNA 17 Dec 2025 -- Russia could support China with military logistics and engage in provocations elsewhere in the Indo-Pacific region if China were to use force against Taiwan, Director-General of Taiwan's National Security Bureau (NSB) Tsai Ming-yen (蔡明彥) said Wednesday.
  • Taiwan seeking highest WHO recognition for curbing hepatitis C: Lai CNA 17 Dec 2025 -- Taiwan will apply for World Health Organization (WHO) certification of its success in preventing and treating hepatitis C by the end of this year, President Lai Ching-te (賴清德) said at a news conference Wednesday.
  • Aircraft carrier Fujian allegedly makes Taiwan Straits transit; likely normal arrangement to boost capability: experts Global Times 17 Dec 2025 -- The defense authority on the island of Taiwan claimed on Wednesday that it spotted the aircraft carrier Fujian of the Chinese People's Liberation Army (PLA) Navy transiting through the Taiwan Straits on Tuesday.

  • Chinese expert warns Japan's rearmament attempt as US announces arms sale to support Japanese Aegis class destroyers Global Times 17 Dec 2025 -- The US announced approval of an arms sale to Japan to provide support for Japanese Aegis class destroyers on Tuesday. Amid Japan's recent consecutive military expansion moves and Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's erroneous remarks on the Taiwan question, a Chinese expert noted that Japan is attempting to rearm itself, undermining the post-war international order, warning that the US should not indulge this trend.

  • C-130J Hercules block upgrade program complete Australia DOD 17 Dec 2025 -- Final Operational Capability (FOC) has been declared for the block upgrade of Australia's C-130J Hercules fleet.
  • Enhancing PNGDF air capability Australia DOD 17 Dec 2025 -- The Royal Australian Air Force's Olgeta Aviator Mobile Training Team - Charlie (MTT-C) have been working side-by-side with the Papua New Guinea Defence Force (PNGDF) Air Transport Wing (ATW), delivering targeted mentoring and capability development across training, operations, maintenance, logistics and personnel management.
  • Training boosts Pacific maritime security Australia DOD 17 Dec 2025 -- Sea Training Unit - Defence Corporation Program (STU-DCP) played a key role in Operation Ika Moana 2025, delivering advanced maritime refresher training to Pacific partner nations under the Pacific Maritime Security Program.

  • S. Korea-UAE Advance Joint Defense Development, Production, and Exports ROK MND 17 Dec 2025 -- The defense ministers of South Korea and the United Arab Emirates (UAE) advance a collaborative model for joint development, local production, and joint export to third countries.
  • "Wartime OPCON Transition to Lead a Stronger Korea and a More Robust ROK-US Alliance" ROK MND 17 Dec 2025 -- On the first anniversary of the Dec. 3 martial law crisis, Defense Minister Ahn Gyu-back stressed the imperative of reconstructing the people's military while safeguarding constitutional principles via deep reflection and drawing vital lessons.
  • "Minister Ahn Vows to Rebuild a People's Military Without Hesitation" ROK MND 17 Dec 2025 -- Defense Minister Ahn Gyu-back declared that egoism and entrenched routines have no place among the military branches in forging a true people's military that is advanced and trusted by the nation.
  • "We Will Respond to the People's Anger and Disappointment with Military Reform" ROK MND 17 Dec 2025 -- On December 2, Defense Minister Ahn Gyu-back reaffirmed his strong commitment to military reform, declaring, "We will address the people's anger and disappointment over the December 3 martial law crisis through sincere reflection, not excuses."

  • INAS 335 (OSPREYS) COMMISSIONED India PIB 17 Dec 2025 -- INAS 335 'The Ospreys', the second Indian Naval Air Squadron to operate MH 60R helicopters, was commissioned at INS Hansa, Goa, in a ceremony presided over by Adm Dinesh K Tripathi, the Chief of the Naval Staff (CNS) on 17 Dec 25
  • ICG Ship Sarthak Makes Strategic Port Call at Chabahar, Iran India PIB 17 Dec 2025 -- The Indian Coast Guard (ICG) Ship Sarthak, an Offshore Patrol Vessel, entered the Chabahar port in Islamic Republic of Iran, a strategically placed deep-water port that offers India a direct maritime gateway to Iran, Afghanistan and Central Asia.
  • INDIAN ARMY CONTINGENT DEPARTS FOR INDIA-UAE JOINT MILITARY EXERCISE DESERT CYCLONE 2025 India PIB 17 Dec 2025 -- An Indian Army contingent has departed for the United Arab Emirates (UAE) to participate in the second edition of the India-UAE Joint Military Exercise DESERT CYCLONE-II, scheduled to be conducted at Abu Dhabi, UAE, from 18 to 30 December 2025.

  • The fourth Hangor class submarine of Pakistan Navy named GHAZI has been launched at Shuangliu Base in Wuhan, China. ISPR Pakistan 17 Dec 2025

  • The Japan-UK Defense Ministerial VTC Ministry of Defense of Japan 17 Dec 2025 -- On December 17, commencing at 17:10 (Japan Time) for approximately 35 minutes, Minister Koizumi held a Defense Ministerial Video Teleconference with Secretary of State for Defence of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the Rt Hon John Healey MP.

  • UK meets with international coalition to keep space safe UK MOD 17 Dec 2025 -- The UK has met with allies as part the Combined Space Operations Initiative (CSpO), a ten-nation space coalition spanning four continents, to discuss the security of space.

  • Finland to sign letter of intent on SAR satellite cooperation with Poland and the Netherlands Finnish MoD 17 Dec 2025 -- Earlier this year, Finland's Minister of Defence Antti Häkkänen authorised the Finnish Defence Forces to procure synthetic aperture radar (SAR) satellites from the Finnish company ICEYE Oy. Finland aims to build international cooperation around the use of these satellites.

  • Memorandum on the acquisition of submarines signed Poland - Ministry of National Defence 17 Dec 2025 -- On 17 December 2025, the deputy prime minister - minister of national defence Władysław Kosiniak-Kamysz and the minister of defence of Sweden Pål Jonson have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) in Warsaw, outlining the main goals of the strategic partnership rooted in the submarines acquisition programme.

  • A look at the Defence news 8 - 14 December Netherlands Ministry of Defence 17 Dec 2025

  • Taskforce Drones tests the technological maturity of attack and defence drones Federal Council - Swiss Government 17 Dec 2025 -- From 8 to 19 December 2025, Swiss attack and defence drones will be tested at the Hinterrhein (GR) shooting range.

  • Acting Defence Minister's meeting with the Belgian Defence Minister, in Sibiu Romania - Ministry of National Defence 17 Dec 2025 -- On Tuesday, December 16th, the Acting Minister of National Defence, Radu Miruță, had an official meeting, at the "Nicolae Bălcescu" Land Forces Academy in Sibiu, with his counterpart from the Kingdom of Belgium, Theo Francken, who is visiting Romania.

  • The first bunkers are being installed in South-East Estonia ECDI 17 Dec 2025 -- st week, preparatory work began to install the first five bunkers in South-East Estonia as part of the creation of the Baltic Defence Line.

  • Next step in Polish submarine procurement Government Offices of Sweden 17 Dec 2025 -- Minister for Defence Pål Jonson and Poland's Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of National Defence Władysław Kosiniak-Kamysz today signed a memorandum of understanding that solidifies the Polish-Swedish cooperation on submarines and lays the foundations for continued negotiations.

  • Russian border guards illegally crossed the temporary control line on the Narva River Estonia MFA 17 Dec 2025 -- This morning, the Estonian Police and Border Guard Board (PPA) detected an illegal crossing of a temporary control line between Estonia and Russia on the Narva River breakwater by three border guards of the Russian Federation.

  • EU Backs 'Military Schengen' to Speed Up Troop Deployments Across Bloc Sputnik 17 Dec 2025 -- The European Parliament adopted on Wednesday a resolution calling for the establishment of a "military Schengen" zone, a framework aimed at ensuring the free and rapid movement of troops and military equipment across European Union borders.

  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Qatari Minister of State Al Khulaifi US Dept. of State 17 Dec 2025
  • Secretary Rubio's Call with UAE Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Sheikh Abdullah bin Zayed Al Nahyan US Dept. of State 17 Dec 2025
  • Secretary Rubio's Meeting with Qatari Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs Al Thani US Dept. of State 17 Dec 2025

Defense Industry

  • Contracts For Dec. 17, 2025

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 17 December 2025 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 17 Dec 2025

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • Aid agencies warn Gaza response at breaking point as Israel urged to lift new restrictions UN News 17 Dec 2025 -- Humanitarian agencies working in the Occupied Palestinian Territory (OPT) have warned that life-saving aid operations risk collapse unless Israel immediately lifts new barriers that are blocking access and forcing international charities to shut down.
  • Statement by the Humanitarian Country Team of the Occupied Palestinian Territory (OPT)* (17 December 2025) UNOCHA 17 Dec 2025 -- UN agencies and NGOs call for immediate lifting of impediments to humanitarian access and NGO operations in the Occupied Palestinian Territory

  • Press release on Deputy Foreign Minister Sergey Vershinin's telephone conversation with General Secretary of the Palestinian National Initiative Mustafa Barghouti Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 17 Dec 2025
  • OIC Hosts a Symposium on "The Palestinian Question: Challenges and Prospects" Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 17 Dec 2025 -- On December 17, 2025, the General Secretariat of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) convened an international symposium titled "The Palestinian Question: Challenges and Prospects

    • Other Conflicts

  • Sanctions, Oversight Mechanisms Risk Losing Credibility Without Results on Ground, Chairs of Subsidiary Bodies Tell Security Council UN Security Council 17 Dec 2025 -- Outgoing Chairs of Security Council subsidiary bodies told the Council today that sanctions and oversight mechanisms can remain credible only if they deliver results on the ground, as they called for stronger implementation, quicker staffing and Chair appointments, and less "business as usual" amid worsening crises from Sudan and Yemen to counter-terrorism and delisting backlogs.

  • Today's top news: Sudan, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Haiti UNOCHA 17 Dec 2025

  • Chinese Defense Ministry spokesperson responds to Philippines' hyping of Xianbin Jiao incident, says Manila puts on 'self-directed and self-staged farces' Global Times 17 Dec 2025 -- In response to the Philippines' distortion of facts and shifting of blame, China's defense ministry spokesperson said on Wednesday that China will continue to take firm and effective measures to resolutely respond to all acts of infringement and provocation.
  • AFP to assist PCG, other agencies in protecting fishers in WPS Philippine News Agency 17 Dec 2025 -- The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) will continue performing its mandate to protect the country's sovereignty, which include supporting efforts to protect Filipino fisherfolk in the West Philippine Sea (WPS).
  • China reaffirms sovereignty over Xianbin Jiao, denounces Philippines' false accusations PRC MND 17 Dec 2025 -- China on Wednesday condemned the Philippines' "thief crying stop thief" behavior after Manila organized vessels to illegally intrude into the lagoon of China's Xianbin Jiao in the South China Sea.

  • Secretary-General's press encounter on Yemen UN Secretary-General 17 Dec 2025
  • Yemen on the brink: Guterres urges restraint, calls for release of UN detainees UN News 17 Dec 2025 -- UN Secretary-General António Guterres called on Yemen's leaders to return to the path of peace and release all UN personnel detained there, following a briefing to the Security Council on Wednesday.

  • ICYMI: Shaheen Secures Repeal of Caesar Act Sanctions on Syria in Annual Defense Bill US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 17 Dec 2025 -- Today, U.S. Senator Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, secured a provision to repeal Caesar sanctions on Syria in the Fiscal Year (FY) 2026 National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA) which now heads to the President's desk for his signature. These sanctions were imposed on Syria during the Assad dictatorship, but since the fall of that regime, have been an impediment to economic development and investment in Syria that would promote the country's stability.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson's Remarks on the Upcoming Shuttle-Diplomacy Trip of MFA Special Envoy for Asian Affairs to Cambodia and Thailand PRC MofA 17 Dec 2025
  • China's past defense co-op with Thailand, Cambodia not linked to their conflict: FM on reports of Chinese-made weapons on battlefield Global Times 17 Dec 2025 -- When responding to a question on reports that Thai military seized Chinese-made anti-tank missiles and other equipment from Cambodian positions during their conflict, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun stressed on Wednesday that China has previously conducted normal defense cooperation with both Thailand and Cambodia, which is not directed at any third party and has no relation to the border conflict between Cambodia and Thailand.

  • DSWD conducts cash-for-work program for 1.4K ex-MILF combatants Philippine News Agency 17 Dec 2025 -- The Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD), through its Peace and Development Buong Bansa Mapayapa Program (PDBBM) and in partnership with the Office of the Presidential Adviser on Peace, Reconciliation and Unity (OPAPRU), conducted a cash-for-work (CFW) payout for 1,433 Moro Islamic Liberation Front (MILF) decommissioned combatants on Wednesday.

  • China Draws Iranian Ire Over UAE Islands Claim RFE/RL 17 Dec 2025 -- China has rattled Tehran by again backing the United Arab Emirates's claim over three Persian Gulf islands controlled by Iran.

  • Sudan: Needs rise as people continue to flee fighting in Kordofan UN News 17 Dec 2025 -- Families continue to flee intense hostilities across the Kordofan region of Sudan and humanitarian needs are surging, the UN said in an update on Wednesday.

  • No Changes Made to Central African Republic Sanctions List, Chair of Committee Established Pursuant to Resolution 2745 (2024) Tells Security Council UN Security Council 17 Dec 2025 -- The Security Council today heard a briefing by Amar Bendjama (Algeria), Chair of its Committee established pursuant to resolution 2745 (2024) concerning the Central African Republic, on the body's activities.

News Reports

  • Trump administration expands list of countries subject to entry restrictions People's Daily 17 Dec 2025 -- U.S. President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed a proclamation expanding the list of countries subject to full or partial entry restrictions to cover around 40 countries, effective on Jan. 1, 2026.

  • House Intel Committee Holds Joint Briefing with Homeland Security Committee in 9/11 Intel Recommendations Review US House of Representatives Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence 17 Dec 2025 -- As part of the bipartisan Review of the 9/11 Commission intelligence recommendations announced on September 11th this year, the House Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence held a closed briefing, joined by the House Committee on Homeland Security, with leaders from the National Counterterrorism Center (NCTC), the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), the Defense Intelligence Agency (DIA), and the Department of Homeland Security (DHS).

  • Sweden Confirms Citizen Detained In Iran On Allegations Of Spying For Israel RFE/RL 17 Dec 2025 -- Sweden has confirmed that one of its citizens is in Iranian custody after Tehran announced that a Swedish national is on trial for allegedly spying for Israel.

  • Barrio Azteca Gang Leader Pleads Guilty to Racketeering Conspiracy and Murder Charges Related to 2010 U.S. Consulate Murders in Juarez US Dept. of Justice 17 Dec 2025 -- A former FBI "Ten Most Wanted Fugitive" and leader of the El Paso/Juarez-based Bario Azteca gang pleaded guilty today in federal court in El Paso, Texas, for his role in the March 2010 U.S. Consulate murders in Juarez, Mexico, in which three people associated with the U.S. Consulate were murdered when gunmen shot into the vehicle in which they and their children were riding.

  • Leader of Transnational Terrorist Group Sentenced to 30 Years in Prison for Soliciting Hate Crimes and Murder, and Conspiring to Provide Material Support to Terrorists US Dept. of Justice 17 Dec 2025 -- Dallas Humber, 35, of Elk Grove, California, — leader of the Terrorgram Collective, a transnational terrorist group — was sentenced today by U.S. District Judge Dena Coggins to 360 months in prison for soliciting hate crimes, soliciting the murder of federal officials, and conspiring to provide material support to terrorists.
  • Secretary Bessent Orders Sanctions Against Violent Mexican Cartel US Treasury 17 Dec 2025 -- Today, the U.S. Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) sanctioned the Cartel de Santa Rosa de Lima (CSRL), which derives the vast majority of its illicit revenue from fuel and oil theft in the Mexican state of Guanajuato. The violent conflict between CSRL and the notorious Mexican terrorist Cartel Jalisco Nuevo Generacion (CJNG) for control of fuel and oil in Guanajuato has made the state one of the deadliest in Mexico. CSRL's activities also help enable a cross-border energy black market, undermine legitimate U.S. oil and natural gas companies, and deprive the Mexican government of critical revenue. OFAC today also sanctioned Jose Antonio Yepez Ortiz, the leader of CSRL.

  • Doorstop - Sydney Prime Minister of Australia 17 Dec 2025
  • PNP probes Bondi Beach shooters' visit to PH Philippine News Agency 17 Dec 2025 -- Philippine National Police acting chief Lt. Gen. Jose Melencio Nartatez Jr. on Wednesday ordered a thorough investigation into the recent visit to the Philippines of the father-and-son suspects linked to the Bondi Beach mass shooting in Sydney, Australia.
  • AFP: Members of Abu Sayyaf, other local terror groups down to 50 Philippine News Agency 17 Dec 2025 -- Members of local terrorist groups (LTGs) in the country have dropped to just about 50 compared to 1,257 nine years ago, the Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) said Wednesday.
  • PBBM rejects PH label as terror hotspot, orders ATC to stay vigilant Philippine News Agency 17 Dec 2025 -- President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr. has directed the Anti-Terrorism Council (ATC) to remain vigilant against possible terror threats, as he rejected foreign media's "misleading" characterization of the Philippines as an "ISIS training hotspot," Malacañang said Wednesday.

  • Deputy Foreign Minister Sergey Vershinin's interview with TASS News Agency ahead of the 2nd Ministerial Conference of the Russia-Africa Partnership Forum (Cairo, December 19˗20, 2025), December 17, 2025 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 17 Dec 2025

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on December 17, 2025 PRC MofA 17 Dec 2025
  • Wang Yi Has a Phone Call with Venezuelan Foreign Minister Yván Gil PRC MofA 17 Dec 2025 -- On December 17, 2025, Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and Foreign Minister Wang Yi had a phone call with Venezuelan Foreign Minister Yván Gil at the latter's request.
  • G7 Foreign Ministers' Statement on the Jimmy Lai verdict in Hong Kong UK FCDO 17 Dec 2025 -- Joint Statement from the G7 Foreign Ministers of Canada, France, Germany, Italy, Japan, the UK, the USA and the High Representative of the EU.
  • HKSAR Security Secretary rebuts editorials from two US media outlets on Jimmy Lai case Global Times 17 Dec 2025 -- Chris Tang Ping-keung, secretary for security of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region (HKSAR) government, on Wednesday wrote letters to editorial departments of The Washington Post and The Wall Street Journal, strongly rebutting the editorial content published by both newspapers on Monday regarding the Jimmy Lai case, Hong Kong media reported. In the letters, Tang emphasized that the articles were severely inconsistent with the facts and reiterated the spirit of the rule of law in Hong Kong, as well as the fairness and transparency of the judicial process.
  • Chinese FM responds to report that UK, France and Germany seek closer China ties as 'blackmail and bargaining chip' against US Global Times 17 Dec 2025 -- In response to a question by Russian media reporter over a report of Russia's Foreign Intelligence Service claiming that the UK, France and Germany are seeking to draw closer to China as a form of "blackmail and bargaining chip" against the US on the issue of Ukraine peace agreement, FM spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Wed that China's position on the Ukraine crisis has been consistent and clear. China supports all efforts conducive to the peaceful resolution of the crisis and hopes that all parties will reach, through dialogue and negotiation, a fair, lasting and binding peace agreement at an early date.
  • Chinese rocket operator responds to SpaceX accusation over 'collision risk'; expert calls on Starlink to share info before frequent maneuvers Global Times 17 Dec 2025 -- SpaceX recently claimed that one of the nine spacecraft that was launched atop China's Kinetica-1 rocket on December 9 zoomed dangerously close to one of its Starlink satellites, accusing the rocket operator for "lack of coordination." In response to the accusation, CAS Space said the incident, if confirmed, occurred nearly 48 hours after payload separation, by which time the launch mission had long concluded.

  • Taiwan made 'essential contributions' at Pax Silica summit: US official CNA 17 Dec 2025 -- Taiwan "was there and made essential contributions" at President Donald Trump's Pax Silica summit on silicon supply chain security issues, a senior American official said Tuesday (U.S. time).
  • Russia could support China attack on Taiwan: Taiwan security chief CNA 17 Dec 2025 -- Russia could support China with military logistics and engage in provocations elsewhere in the Indo-Pacific region if China were to use force against Taiwan, Director-General of Taiwan's National Security Bureau (NSB) Tsai Ming-yen (蔡明彥) said Wednesday.
  • Taiwan seeking highest WHO recognition for curbing hepatitis C: Lai CNA 17 Dec 2025 -- Taiwan will apply for World Health Organization (WHO) certification of its success in preventing and treating hepatitis C by the end of this year, President Lai Ching-te (賴清德) said at a news conference Wednesday.
  • Aircraft carrier Fujian allegedly makes Taiwan Straits transit; likely normal arrangement to boost capability: experts Global Times 17 Dec 2025 -- The defense authority on the island of Taiwan claimed on Wednesday that it spotted the aircraft carrier Fujian of the Chinese People's Liberation Army (PLA) Navy transiting through the Taiwan Straits on Tuesday.
  • China urges Japan to correct its mistakes on Taiwan-related remarks, warning of militarism revival Global Times 17 Dec 2025 -- China continued to slam the erroneous remarks from Japanese side on the Taiwan question on Wednesday, warning that the dangerous trend of a Japanese militarism revival has drawn high vigilance from countries and peoples in the region.
  • A first: Taiwan spots new PLA aircraft carrier sailing in Taiwan Strait CNA 17 Dec 2025 -- Taiwan's military has spotted China's new aircraft carrier, the Fujian, for the first time since it was commissioned making a transit through the Taiwan Strait and released a surveillance photograph of it.
  • Taiwan question purely China's internal affair, brooking no external interference, mainland spokesperson rebukes Japanese PM's erroneous claim Global Times 17 Dec 2025 -- The Taiwan question is purely China's internal affair, and resolving it is a matter for the Chinese people themselves, brooking no external interference. Japanese side should earnestly reflect on and correct its mistakes, and withdraw its erroneous remarks, Zhu Fenglian, spokesperson for the State Council Taiwan Affairs Office, made the remakes at a regular conference on Wednesday, when asked to comment on Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi recent claim during questioning in the House of Councilors saying that the Taiwan question should be resolved peacefully through dialogue.
  • DPP's deliberate move to ban Xiaohongshu 'political manipulation' with real aim of depriving people's right to info: spokesperson Global Times 17 Dec 2025 -- Commenting on the DPP authorities' one-year "ban" on social media platform Xiaohongshu (RedNote), a spokesperson for the State Council Taiwan Affairs Office said on Wednesday the deliberate and heavy-handed move is a clear example of "double standards" and political manipulation.
  • Taiwan politician's remarks 'even in an event of war, Taiwan island could maintain normal life like Ukraine' are utterly inhumane: spokesperson Global Times 17 Dec 2025 -- Zhu Fenglian, a spokesperson for the Taiwan Affairs Office of the State Council said on Wednesday, slammed pro-Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) politician's words as utterly inhumane, in response to a question on Taipei city councilor Miao Po-ya's recent remarks that even in an event of war, Taiwan island could maintain normal life like Ukraine, where school and work could "continue as usual," which caused an uproar in public opinion with netizens on the island condemning her for glorifying war.
  • Mainland spokesperson slams WSJ PLA report, blames Lai Ching-te authorities' separatism for escalation Global Times 17 Dec 2025 -- Resolving the Taiwan question is an internal affair that belongs to the Chinese people alone, brooking absolutely no external interference whatsoever. The current complex and severe situation in the Taiwan Straits is fundamentally due to the Lai Ching-te authorities' stubborn adherence to the "Taiwan independence" separatist stance and their continuous collusion with external forces to provoke "independence" actions, mainland spokesperson Zhu Fenglian commented on Wednesday
  • Mainland spokesperson slams DPP's 'investigation' on 'pro-mainland' entertainers, stresses cross-Starts exchanges unstoppable Global Times 17 Dec 2025 -- The DPP authorities' use of so-called "investigations" to threaten and intimidate entertainers from the Taiwan region goes completely against the public sentiments and cannot stop the momentum of people from Taiwan participating in cross-Straits exchanges and sharing the development opportunities on the mainland, Zhu Fenglian, spokesperson for the State Council Taiwan Affairs Office, told a regular conference on Wednesday
  • Taiwan question purely China's internal affair, brooking no external interference, mainland spokesperson rebukes Japanese PM's erroneous claim Global Times 17 Dec 2025 -- The Taiwan question is purely China's internal affair, and resolving it is a matter for the Chinese people themselves, brooking no external interference. Japanese side should earnestly reflect on and correct its mistakes, and withdraw its erroneous remarks, Zhu Fenglian, spokesperson for the State Council Taiwan Affairs Office, made the remakes at a regular conference on Wednesday,.
  • Washington should avoid 'Taiwan independence' forces dragging China, US into dangerous situation: spokesperson on US' new NSS Global Times 17 Dec 2025 -- The US side should abide by the one-China principle and the provisions of the three China-US joint communiqués, handle Taiwan-related issues with prudence, and avoid allowing a very small number of "Taiwan independence" separatists to drag China and the US into dangerous situation of conflict and confrontation, Zhu Fenglian, a spokesperson for the State Council Taiwan Affairs Office, said on Wednesday
  • Ministry of National Defense Issues Press Release Regarding"PLA's Joint Combat Readiness Patrol" ROC MND 17 Dec 2025
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 17 Dec 2025

  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un Visits Kumsusan Palace of Sun KCNA 17 Dec 2025 -- All the people and service personnel across the DPRK pay the highest tribute, with boundless yearning and ardent loyalty, to Chairman Kim Jong Il , looking back with deep emotion on the sacred revolutionary career and eternal exploits of the peerlessly great man, who set it as his lifelong cause to build on this land a country which will always be powerful and prosperous and devoted his all to its realization, on the occasion of the 14th anniversary of his demise.

  • Iran appoints new air defense, air force commanders IRNA 17 Dec 2025 -- The Leader of the Islamic Revolution has appointed new commanders for the country's air defense headquarters and the Army's air force.
  • Iran nuclear issue has no military solution: Foreign Minister Araghchi IRNA 17 Dec 2025 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says Iran's nuclear issue has no military solution, citing attacks by the United States and Israel against Iranian nuclear sites in June, which he said failed to eliminate nuclear technology in the Islamic Republic.
  • US-Israeli spy network neutralized during 12 day war: Iran Armed Forces Press TV 17 Dec 2025 -- The senior spokesman for the Iranian Armed Forces says the enemy's extensive espionage network was dealt a severe blow during the 12-day Israeli-US aggression in June and largely dismantled, describing it as a major intelligence achievement.
  • Iran has 'complete' intelligence database on Israeli targets: IRGC IRNA 18 Dec 2025 -- A senior spokesperson for the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) says Iran had built up a complete intelligence database on Israeli targets prior to the 12-day imposed war of June, enabling Iranian missiles to strike with precise accuracy.
  • FM Araghchi: Iran-Russia 'strategic partnership' basis for multilateral order Press TV 17 Dec 2025 -- Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has described relations between Tehran and Moscow as a "strategic partnership" aimed at advancing a new, just, and genuinely multilateral world order.
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's opening remarks at talks with Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Islamic Republic of Iran Abbas Araghchi, Moscow, December 17, 2025 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 17 Dec 2025
  • Russia Ready to Help Iran Find Solutions to Nuclear Program Crisis - Lavrov Sputnik 17 Dec 2025 -- Russia is ready to assist Iran in the search of a suitable settlement of the crisis connected with its nuclear program, Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov said on Wednesday.

  • Remarks by PM Netanyahu and Energy and Infrastructure Minister Eli Cohen following approval Gas Export Deal to Egypt Israel - Prime Minister 17 Dec 2025

  • Belarus: Freeing political prisoners does not end policy of repression, warns UN expert OHCHR 17 Dec 2025 -- A UN expert today welcomed the recent releases of prisoners convicted in relation to protests around the 2020 presidential elections but cautioned against excessive optimism about the human rights situation in Belarus.
  • Washington And Brussels Play Good Cop, Bad Cop On Belarus RFE/RL 17 Dec 2025 -- Recent moves by Washington and Brussels show diplomatic tracks heading in opposite directions with regard to Belarus, highlighting a growing divide between the allies on how to deal with Europe's security.

  • Department of Space - Year End Review 2025 India PIB 17 Dec 2025
  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: MAJOR SPACE MISSION BY ISRO India PIB 17 Dec 2025 -- During the last 5 years (December 2020 to December 2025), 22 ISRO satellites have been launched
  • Transcript of Special Briefing by MEA on Prime Minister's Visit to Ethiopia (December 17, 2025) India MEA 17 Dec 2025
  • Dr. Jitendra Singh Says SHANTI Bill Retains Strong Safety and Liability Safeguards Amid Lok Sabha Debate India PIB 17 Dec 2025 -- Union Minister of Science and Technology Dr
  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: APPLICATION OF ATOMIC ENERGY TECHNOLOGIES IN UTTAR PRADESH India PIB 17 Dec 2025 -- Bhabha Atomic Research Centre (BARC) a constituent Unit of Department of Atomic Energy (DAE), is actively involved in improvement of local aromatic rice landrace 'Kalanamak' using radiation induced mutation breeding in collaboration with Acharya Narendra Dev University of Agriculture and Technology (ANDUAT), Kumarganj, Ayodhya
  • PALRIAMENT QUESTION: NUCLEAR ENERGY CAPACITY India PIB 17 Dec 2025 -- The present installed nuclear power capacity is 8780 MW (excluding RAPS-1, 100 MW) and the nuclear electricity generated during 2024-25 is 56681 million Units
  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: USE OF NUCLEAR TECHNOLOGY FOR WATER PURIFICATION IN FLOOD-AFFECTED AREAS India PIB 17 Dec 2025 -- Bhabha Atomic Research Centre (BARC) has developed cost-effective and location/contaminant-specific membrane-based water purification technologies, as a spin-off of its in-house technology development initiatives, which can find application in treatment of water in flood affected areas
  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: NUCLEAR MISSION India PIB 17 Dec 2025
  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: INCLUSION OF NUCLEAR ENERGY India PIB 17 Dec 2025 -- Nuclear Energy has a huge potential to ensure long term energy security
  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: NUCLEAR SCIENCE FOR AGRICULTURE AND FOOD PRESERVATION India PIB 17 Dec 2025

  • Diplomacy, the Australian Way Australia DFAT 17 Dec 2025

  • Launch of the UK's new Approach to Africa: Baroness Chapman's speech UK FCDO 17 Dec 2025 -- Baroness Chapman launches the UK's new Approach to Africa in a speech in London to African Ambassadors and High Commissioners, UK parliamentarians, and Africa policy shapers in civil society and the private sector.

  • Foreign Affairs Council, 15 December 2025 EC/CEU 17 Dec 2025

  • Statement by President António Costa following the EU-Western Balkans summit EC/CEU 17 Dec 2025
  • Statement by President von der Leyen following the EU-Western Balkans Summit European Commission 17 Dec 2025
  • The President: EU enlargement to the Western Balkans is a geostrategic investment in the security of Europe President of the Republic of Lithuania 17 Dec 2025 -- In Brussels, President Gitanas Nausėda took part in the European Union-Western Balkans Summit, which focused on strengthening the strategic partnership between the EU and the Western Balkans, the stability and security of the region, and the progress of the Western Balkan countries' integration into the European Union.

  • Bangladesh High Commissioner to India summoned by the Ministry of External Affairs India MEA 17 Dec 2025 -- The Bangladesh High Commissioner to India, H.E. Mr Riaz Hamidullah, was today (December 17, 2025) summoned by the Ministry of External Affairs and apprised of India's strong concerns at the deteriorating security environment in Bangladesh.

  • World News in Brief: Progress on hunger in Asia-Pacific, key Gaza pipeline repaired, flu hits Europe hard UN News 17 Dec 2025 -- While the Asia and Pacific region has made notable progress in reducing hunger, persistent challenges remain in addressing malnutrition, food insecurity and unequal access to healthy diets, a new UN report published on Wednesday concludes.
